macro_summary: U.S. equities dipped on Wednesday as the Federal Reserve raised its year-end inflation forecast to 3.6% and penciled in a potential rate hike, overshadowing the ongoing oil retreat from the Iran peace deal. WTI settled at $74.36, down 15% over five sessions, while the VIX eased to 17.0 ahead of Chair Warsh's first FOMC decision due today. Tech was hit hardest — QQQ fell 1.0% — as the hawkish dot-plot dampened AI optimism.
commodities_note: WTI edged 0.2% lower to $74.36, extending its post-Hormuz-deal collapse. Gold lost 0.8% to $4,305 as real yields firmed. The 10-year Treasury yield ticked up to 4.46% ahead of the FOMC conclusion.

support_legs: Q1 2026 revenue +58% YoY confirming commercial fleet licensing model (per WeRide IR, May 13 2026)
Expanding multi-country robotaxi deployment (Uber in Zurich, Madrid; Grab in Southeast Asia) with regulatory momentum
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if WRD fails to reclaim SMA50 ($7.41) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

writeup.headline: SITM 63x forward PE, 5.9% above SMA50 — blocked by valuation.
writeup.what_to_do: The MEMS timing displacement thesis is valid, but 63x forward PE with no FCF and a 5.9% extension leaves no margin for error. The wide-stop R:R to $609.15 support is 3.3:1, but that's academic without a valuation re-rating. Avoid until growth justifies the multiple or the stock pulls back to the 50-day.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Co-recipient of NVDA's $2B photonics investment alongside LITE — reinforces the AI optical interconnect theme. A LITE/COHR basket confirmation on any photonics catalyst day.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's entry setup triggers and COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC designs for Amazon Trainium and Google Axion, directly competitive with AVGO. Hyperscaler ASIC spend trajectory is a first-order revenue read for both.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch after AVGO's next earnings for a read on custom silicon wallet share; revisit if MRVL dips 8%.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM supplier after SK Hynix and Micron. Samsung's HBM3E qualification status with NVDA will determine memory supply diversity and pricing power for the whole HBM complex.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if SK Hynix's extreme extension cracks on the Korea Friday session and Samsung HBM qualification news surfaces.

active_risks: US-Iran peace deal implementation: Strait of Hormuz reopening is underway but infrastructure damage and Iran's proposed 'service fees' add residual oil risk.
US-China tech blacklist: more than 100 firms, including DeepSeek, await Entity List additions, while Bosch was fined $36M for unauthorized Huawei shipments (per Reuters, Jun 17).
Trump tariff reinvigoration: 100% wine tariff threat on France, auto tariffs loom; US-Mexico-Canada trade deal under pressure.
EU weakness and ECB hawkishness: Bundesbank cuts growth outlook, French debt storm brews, ECB signals a potential rate hike despite slowing growth.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $74 has eased inflation fears but not enough to deter hawkish Fed dot-plots. The semis cluster surging to +21% over the month is now confronting a possible rate hike. Singapore banks and AI power names like BE are still priced for a world of sustained high macro uncertainty.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: FOMC meeting begins under Chair Warsh with hawkish SEP signaling a hike. Bosch fined $36M for Entity List violations (per Reuters, Jun 17). Oil continues to slide as Hormuz reopens, hitting a three-month low.

base.description: Peace deal holds, Hormuz fully reopens over weeks, WTI stabilises near $75. Fed holds but keeps a hawkish bias; rate hike expectation lingers. Semis remain extended but no crash; NVDA and AVGO stabilise.

optimistic.description: Oil drops below $70 as supply normalises faster than expected; Fed becomes less hawkish, enabling a relief rally in tech. Extended semis like MU and AMD consolidate peacefully, opening entries.

pessimistic.description: Hormuz deal collapses or Iran imposes prohibitive fees, WTI spikes back above $95. Fed forced to hike. Semis sell off 15-20%; Singapore banks tumble on renewed inflation fears.

wildcard.description: China imposes retaliatory export controls on rare earths or restricts AI chip supplies, triggering a sector-wide shock that bypasses oil dynamics.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Keep cash heavy: only WRD qualifies for a starter position. Deploy nothing else until the FOMC outcome clarifies the rate path.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive with a single ACCUMULATE spark
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 14 of 28 names are hard-blocked on technical extension. The extreme euphoria in semis and Singapore banks leaves almost no clean entry, while the FOMC decision today is the critical macro pivot that could either relieve or crush the blockade.

Taiwan AI infrastructure partnerships; CoWoS capacity commitments from TSMC; HBM4 roadmap. Direct thesis-confirming event for AI Power Infrastructure cluster.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt.
